# obsidian-livesync-mcp

> A standalone MCP (Model Context Protocol) HTTP server, written in Go, that lets
> AI agents read and write an Obsidian vault that is synced by Self-hosted
> LiveSync. The Go server does plain-filesystem CRUD on a vault directory; a
> supervised `livesync-cli` daemon (Node) keeps that directory continuously
> synced with a remote CouchDB.

This file is a setup guide for AI agents installing the project. Prefer the
Docker path — it is the only one that does not require manually building
`livesync-cli`, which is NOT published to npm.

## Requirements

- **Docker + Docker Compose** (recommended), OR
- **Go 1.26+**, a from-source `livesync-cli`, and a reachable CouchDB configured
  as a LiveSync remote.

## Install & run — Docker (recommended)

```bash
git clone https://github.com/adambenhassen/obsidian-livesync-mcp
cd obsidian-livesync-mcp
docker compose up --build -d        # CouchDB + from-source livesync-cli + MCP server
curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer changeme" http://localhost:8765/healthz   # expect HTTP 200
```

- MCP endpoint: `POST http://localhost:8765/mcp` (MCP Streamable HTTP).
- Auth: `Authorization: Bearer changeme` — change `MCP_API_KEY` in `docker-compose.yml`.
- CouchDB credentials/db are set in `docker-compose.yml` (`admin`/`password`, db `livesync`).

## Install & run — local (advanced)

1. Build `livesync-cli` from https://github.com/vrtmrz/obsidian-livesync:
   `git submodule update --init --recursive`, `npm install`,
   `cd src/apps/cli && npm run build` (launcher = `node dist/index.cjs`).
2. Prepare a database dir whose `.livesync/settings.json` is configured for your
   CouchDB (`couchDB_URI`, `couchDB_USER`, `couchDB_PASSWORD`, `couchDB_DBNAME`,
   `isConfigured: true`).
3. Run:

```bash
LIVESYNC_VAULT=/path/to/vault \
LIVESYNC_DB=/path/to/db \
LIVESYNC_CLI=/path/to/livesync-cli \
LIVESYNC_INTERVAL=5 \
MCP_API_KEY=secret \
  go run ./cmd/livesync-mcp
```

## Configuration (environment variables)

| Variable           | Required | Default          | Notes |
|--------------------|----------|------------------|-------|
| `LIVESYNC_VAULT`   | yes      | —                | Vault dir of `.md` files |
| `LIVESYNC_DB`      | yes      | —                | livesync-cli database dir (single-process) |
| `LIVESYNC_CLI`     | no       | `livesync-cli`   | Path to the CLI launcher |
| `LIVESYNC_INTERVAL`| no       | `0`              | Daemon CouchDB poll seconds. **Must be > 0 for sync to occur.** |
| `MCP_ADDR`         | no       | `127.0.0.1:8765` | HTTP listen address |
| `MCP_API_KEY`      | no       | _(empty)_        | Bearer token; empty disables auth |

## MCP tools

`list_notes(folder?, recursive?)`, `read_note(path)`,
`write_note(path, content, overwrite?)`, `append_to_note(path, content)`,
`delete_note(path)`, `move_note(from, to)`, `search_notes(query, mode)`,
`get_note_metadata(path)`. Paths are vault-relative and forward-slashed;
absolute paths, `..` traversal, and symlinks escaping the vault are rejected.

## Important notes (read before deploying against real data)

- `livesync-cli` is **not on npm**; it must be built from source. The Docker
  image does this for you.
- Sync requires `LIVESYNC_INTERVAL > 0` (the Docker image sets `5`). Without it
  the daemon connects but does not replicate.
- The database dir is **single-process**: never run another `livesync-cli`
  command against `LIVESYNC_DB` while the server is running.
- **Data safety:** pointing a fresh instance (empty vault, new db) at a CouchDB
  that already contains a vault **pulls the existing data down** — it does not
  delete the remote. Verified by `TestExistingRemoteDataIsPreserved` in
  `test/integration_test.go`.
- Deletes propagate as LiveSync **soft-delete tombstones** (the CouchDB doc body
  gains `"deleted": true`; the doc is not removed via CouchDB native `_deleted`).

## Verify

- Health: `GET /healthz` → `200` when the sync daemon is up, `503` when down.
- Full end-to-end (real CouchDB, in Docker):
  `docker compose --profile test run --rm e2e-test`

## Source layout

- `cmd/livesync-mcp/` — entrypoint (config, daemon supervision, HTTP wiring).
- `internal/vault/` — path-safe filesystem CRUD + search.
- `internal/daemon/` — supervises `livesync-cli <db> daemon --vault <vault> --interval N`.
- `internal/mcpserver/` — MCP tool registration.
- `internal/auth/` — bearer-token middleware.
- `deploy/`, `Dockerfile`, `docker-compose.yml` — the runnable stack.
- `test/integration_test.go` — gated end-to-end tests (build tag `integration`).
